# Financial Model

*5-year financial projections for investor discussions.*
*All figures sourced from [financial-framework.md](../financial-framework.md)
and [market-sizing-and-pricing-validation.md](../market-sizing-and-pricing-validation.md).*

*An Excel version of this model can be generated by running
`python scripts/generate_financial_model.py`.*

---

## 1. Revenue Model

### Pricing Structure

| Tier | Firm Revenue | Headcount | Monthly Platform | Annual Setup | ACV (Platform Only) |
|------|-------------|-----------|-----------------|-------------|---------------------|
| Starter | $2M-$10M | 10-30 | $2,000 | $7,500 | $24,000 |
| Growth | $10M-$25M | 30-75 | $3,500 | $10,000 | $42,000 |
| Scale | $25M-$50M | 75-200 | $5,000 | $15,000 | $60,000 |

Optional tuning retainer adds $1,500/mo ($18K/year) for firms that want ongoing support.

### Revenue Buildup -- Moderate Scenario

| Quarter | New Firms | Total Firms | Tier Mix (S/G/Sc) | New Setup Rev | Quarterly MRR Growth | End MRR | Quarterly Rev |
|---------|-----------|-------------|--------------------|--------------|---------------------|---------|---------------|
| Q3 2026 | 1 | 1 | 1/0/0 | $7,500 | $2,000 | $2,000 | $11,500 |
| Q4 2026 | 2 | 3 | 2/1/0 | $17,500 | $5,500 | $7,500 | $35,000 |
| Q1 2027 | 3 | 6 | 3/2/1 | $30,000 | $10,500 | $18,000 | $78,000 |
| Q2 2027 | 4 | 10 | 4/4/2 | $40,000 | $14,000 | $32,000 | $128,000 |
| Q3 2027 | 5 | 15 | 6/6/3 | $52,500 | $17,500 | $49,500 | $183,000 |
| Q4 2027 | 5 | 20 | 8/8/4 | $52,500 | $17,500 | $67,000 | $236,500 |
| Q1 2028 | 5 | 25 | 10/10/5 | $52,500 | $17,500 | $84,500 | $292,500 |
| Q2 2028 | 5 | 30 | 12/12/6 | $52,500 | $17,500 | $102,000 | $351,000 |
| Q3 2028 | 5 | 35 | 14/14/7 | $52,500 | $17,500 | $119,500 | $411,000 |
| Q4 2028 | 5 | 40 | 16/16/8 | $52,500 | $17,500 | $137,000 | $463,500 |

### Annual Revenue Summary -- Three Scenarios

| Year | Conservative Firms | Conservative ARR | Moderate Firms | Moderate ARR | Aggressive Firms | Aggressive ARR |
|------|-------------------|-----------------|----------------|-------------|-----------------|---------------|
| 2026 (H2) | 2 | $48K | 3 | $90K | 3 | $90K |
| 2027 | 8 | $240K | 15 | $570K | 20 | $800K |
| 2028 | 18 | $612K | 40 | $1,680K | 60 | $2,760K |
| 2029 | 30 | $1,140K | 80 | $3,520K | 150 | $7,500K |
| 2030 | 45 | $1,800K | 140 | $6,440K | 300 | $16,200K |
| 2031 | 65 | $2,730K | 220 | $10,560K | 500 | $29,000K |

---

## 2. Cost Structure

### Fixed Monthly Costs

| Category | Phase 0-1 (Pre-Revenue) | Phase 2 (1-20 Firms) | Phase 3 (20+ Firms) |
|----------|------------------------|---------------------|---------------------|
| Cursor licenses | $220 | $220 | $500 |
| Collaboration tools | $15 | $50 | $100 |
| Cloud infrastructure | $50 | $500 | $2,000 |
| Domain / email | $20 | $20 | $50 |
| LLM API costs | $0 | $200 | $1,000 |
| Software tools | $50 | $200 | $500 |
| **Total fixed** | **$355** | **$1,190** | **$4,150** |

### Variable Costs (Per Firm Per Month)

| Component | Phase 1 (MVP) | Phase 2-3 (Production) |
|-----------|---------------|----------------------|
| Compute hosting | $20 | $100 |
| Data lakehouse | $0 | $150 |
| Vector database | $0 | $50 |
| LLM inference | $25 | $25 |
| Monitoring | $0 | $25 |
| **Total per firm** | **$45** | **$350** |

### People Costs

| Role | When | Annual Cost | Monthly |
|------|------|-------------|---------|
| Mike (no salary from company) | Stage 1 | $0 | $0 |
| Owen (part-time retention) | Stage 1 funding | $12,000 | $1,000 |
| Mike (salary begins) | Stage 2 funding | $72,000 | $6,000 |
| Owen (expanded to 20+ hrs) | Stage 2 funding | $48,000 | $4,000 |
| First engineer | Seed funding | $100,000 | $8,333 |
| Second engineer | Seed + revenue | $100,000 | $8,333 |
| Customer success | Seed + revenue | $70,000 | $5,833 |

Note: During Stage 1, Mike takes no salary from the company. Personal expenses ($4K/month) are covered by the W-2 salary (Parr3) and ML Financial Group income ($30K/year). The founder already self-funds the entire business at ~$750-$1,200/month — Owen at $115/week (5 hrs, authorized to double to 10 hrs at $230/week), AI tools (~$200/month), subscriptions (~$75/month). Owen's Stage 1 line item ($1K/month) formalizes his existing compensation under the entity. Mike begins drawing salary at Stage 2 when the company has revenue and growth capital.

---

## 3. Unit Economics

### Per-Firm Economics

| Metric | Starter Tier | Growth Tier | Scale Tier |
|--------|-------------|------------|------------|
| Monthly revenue | $2,000 | $3,500 | $5,000 |
| Variable cost (Phase 2) | $350 | $350 | $350 |
| Contribution margin | $1,650 | $3,150 | $4,650 |
| Contribution margin % | 82.5% | 90.0% | 93.0% |
| Setup revenue | $7,500 | $10,000 | $15,000 |
| Setup cost (Mike's time, ~30 hrs @ $60/hr) | $1,800 | $1,800 | $1,800 |
| Setup margin | $5,700 | $8,200 | $13,200 |
| Annual customer value | $29,700 | $47,800 | $73,200 |
| Annual cost to serve | $6,000 | $6,000 | $6,000 |
| Annual gross profit per firm | $23,700 | $41,800 | $67,200 |

### LTV / CAC (Projected)

| Metric | Value | Basis |
|--------|-------|-------|
| Average customer lifetime | 5+ years | Vertical SaaS retention benchmarks; switching costs > $500K |
| Annual churn (target) | < 10% | Vertical SaaS leaders average < 8% |
| Blended annual gross profit | $40K | Weighted by tier mix |
| Customer LTV | $200K+ | 5 years x $40K gross profit |
| Target CAC (founder-led) | $5K-$10K | Mike's time + travel + demo costs |
| Target CAC (scaled) | $15K-$25K | Sales team + marketing + events |
| LTV/CAC ratio | 8-20x | Well above the 3x minimum threshold |

---

## 4. Cash Flow -- Monthly (First 24 Months)

### Pre-Funding (Current through Stage 1 close)

Monthly burn: ~$750-$1,200 (Owen at $460-$920/mo + AI tools ~$200/mo + subscriptions ~$75/mo -- entirely self-funded by founder)

### Post-Stage 1 Funding ($50K Friends Round received, Month 0 = funding close)

| Month | Revenue | People Cost | Infra Cost | GTM Cost | Legal/Other | Net Cash Flow | Cash Balance |
|-------|---------|-------------|------------|----------|-------------|--------------|-------------|
| 0 | $0 | $0 | $300 | $0 | $10,000 | -$10,300 | $39,700 |
| 1 | $0 | $1,000 | $300 | $0 | $3,000 | -$4,300 | $35,400 |
| 2 | $0 | $1,000 | $300 | $0 | $1,000 | -$2,300 | $33,100 |
| 3 | $0 | $1,000 | $300 | $0 | $500 | -$1,800 | $31,300 |
| 4 | $0 | $1,000 | $350 | $3,000 | $0 | -$4,350 | $26,950 |
| 5 | $0 | $1,000 | $350 | $0 | $0 | -$1,350 | $25,600 |
| 6 | $9,500 | $1,000 | $400 | $0 | $0 | $8,100 | $33,700 |
| 7 | $2,000 | $1,000 | $400 | $0 | $0 | $600 | $34,300 |
| 8 | $2,000 | $1,000 | $400 | $3,000 | $0 | -$2,400 | $31,900 |
| 9 | $11,500 | $1,000 | $450 | $0 | $0 | $10,050 | $41,950 |
| 10 | $5,500 | $1,000 | $450 | $0 | $0 | $4,050 | $46,000 |
| 11 | $5,500 | $1,000 | $450 | $0 | $0 | $4,050 | $50,050 |
| 12 | $17,500 | $1,000 | $500 | $0 | $0 | $16,000 | $66,050 |

**Key assumptions (Stage 1):**
- Mike takes no salary from the company; personal expenses covered by W-2 + practice income
- People cost: $1,000/month Owen only
- Infra cost: ~$275/month (AI tools ~$200, hosting, subscriptions ~$75) — already being paid by founder; now covered by company
- Month 0: Legal/entity costs ($10K attorney + formation + insurance)
- Month 1: Additional legal ($3K IP assignment, advisor agreements)
- Month 4, 8: Conference attendance ($3K each)
- Month 6: First paying customer (Starter tier) with $7,500 setup fee
- Month 9: Second customer (Growth tier) with $10,000 setup fee
- Month 12: Third customer (Starter tier) with $7,500 setup fee
- By month 6, the company is cash-flow positive on a monthly basis
- By month 12, the company has more cash than it started with ($66K vs $50K)

**Runway on Stage 1 alone:** At ~$1,300-$1,800/month burn (Owen + infra, pre-revenue), $50K provides 28-38 months of runway before any revenue. With revenue starting month 6, the capital is essentially permanent — the business funds itself. The $50K raise is not survival capital; it is a legal and growth foundation.

**If Parr3 invests $75K instead of $50K:** Additional $25K provides a larger reserve, enables a third conference, or funds an early engineering contractor. Cash at month 12 would be ~$91K.

### Post-Stage 2 Funding ($400K Pre-Seed received, ~Month 9-12)

Stage 2 capital arrives when 1-3 customers are paying. The cash flow model shifts to growth mode:

| Month (from Stage 2 close) | Revenue | People Cost | Infra Cost | GTM Cost | Other | Net Cash Flow | Cash Balance |
|---------------------------|---------|-------------|------------|----------|-------|--------------|-------------|
| 0 | $5,500 | $2,500 | $490 | $0 | $2,000 | $510 | $400,510 |
| 1 | $5,500 | $10,000 | $500 | $3,000 | $500 | -$8,500 | $392,010 |
| 2 | $5,500 | $10,000 | $500 | $3,000 | $500 | -$8,500 | $383,510 |
| 3 | $15,500 | $10,000 | $550 | $5,000 | $500 | -$550 | $382,960 |
| 6 | $18,000 | $10,000 | $650 | $5,000 | $500 | $1,850 | ~$365K |
| 9 | $24,500 | $10,000 | $750 | $5,000 | $500 | $8,250 | ~$365K |
| 12 | $35,000 | $10,000 | $900 | $5,000 | $500 | $18,600 | ~$385K |

**Key assumptions (Stage 2):**
- People cost increases to ~$10K/month (Owen at 20+ hrs, founder salary increase, contractors)
- GTM spend increases for conferences, sales assets, content
- Revenue accelerates with dedicated execution capacity
- By month 12 of Stage 2: ~10 firms, ~$35K MRR, cash-flow positive and growing
- Seed round expected around this point ($1M-$2M at $10M-$20M valuation)

**Combined runway:** Stage 1 + Stage 2 capital provides 24+ months of runway even in the slow-acquisition scenario. Seed round expected before Stage 2 capital is exhausted.

---

## 5. Sensitivity Analysis

### Scenario: Slower Customer Acquisition

If customers take 50% longer to close (9 months between customers instead of 3):

| Month | Firms | MRR | Cash Balance |
|-------|-------|-----|-------------|
| 6 | 0 | $0 | $288,835 |
| 9 | 1 | $2,000 | $235,455 |
| 12 | 1 | $2,000 | $185,460 |
| 15 | 2 | $5,500 | $148,870 |
| 18 | 2 | $5,500 | $115,095 |

Still has 6+ months of runway at month 18. Seed round urgency increases but is not critical.

### Scenario: Higher Burn (Full-Time Engineer Added Before Stage 2)

Adding a $100K/year engineer before Stage 2 capital arrives:

| Additional monthly cost | Impact on Stage 1 runway |
|------------------------|--------------------------|
| $8,333/mo | Exhausts Stage 1 capital within ~8 months |
| Requires Stage 2 by month 6 | Tight; forces premature fundraise |

Recommendation: Do not add a full-time engineer before Stage 2 funding closes. Stage 1 is designed for lean execution.

### Scenario: ACV Lower Than Expected ($24K instead of $40K blended)

| Metric | Original | 40% Lower ACV |
|--------|----------|---------------|
| Year 2 ARR (moderate) | $570K | $342K |
| Year 3 ARR (moderate) | $1,680K | $1,008K |
| Seed valuation impact | $8-15M | $5-10M |

Even at 40% lower ACV, the unit economics remain profitable (82%+ gross margin on Starter tier). Valuation takes a hit but the business is still viable.

---

## 6. Cap Table Model

### Stage 1: Friends Round ($50K SAFE at $3.5M post-money cap) + Advisor Grants

| Holder | Pre-SAFE | Post-SAFE (at conversion) |
|--------|----------|--------------------------|
| Mike Leavenworth | 75.0% | 72.7% |
| Owen | 25.0% | 24.2% |
| Advisors (6 x 0.25%) | 0.0% | 1.48% |
| Friends Round investors | 0.0% | 1.43% |
| **Total** | **100%** | **100%** |

Note: SAFE conversion happens at the next priced round. Until then, SAFE holders have no equity -- they have a right to receive equity at the cap when the next priced round occurs. Friends-round investors get $50K/$3.5M = 1.43%. If Parr3 invests $75K, their position would be 2.14%.

### Stage 2: Pre-Seed ($400K SAFE at $7M post-money cap)

| Holder | Post-Pre-Seed |
|--------|---------------|
| Mike Leavenworth | 68.2% |
| Owen | 22.7% |
| Advisors | 1.39% |
| Friends Round investors (Stage 1) | 2.02% |
| Pre-Seed investors (Stage 2) | 5.71% |
| **Total** | **100%** |

### Stage 3: Seed ($1.5M at $15M pre-money, 10% option pool)

| Holder | Post-Seed |
|--------|-----------|
| Mike Leavenworth | 55.2% |
| Owen | 18.4% |
| Advisors | 1.12% |
| Friends Round investors (Stage 1) | 1.63% |
| Pre-Seed investors (Stage 2) | 4.62% |
| Seed investors (Stage 3) | 9.09% |
| Employee option pool | 10.0% |
| **Total** | **100%** |

### Series A (Illustrative, $5M at $35M pre-money)

| Holder | Post-Series A |
|--------|--------------|
| Mike Leavenworth | 48.3% |
| Owen | 16.1% |
| Advisors | 0.98% |
| Friends Round investors | 1.43% |
| Pre-Seed investors | 4.04% |
| Seed investors | 7.95% |
| Series A investors | 12.5% |
| Option pool (expanded) | 8.7% |
| **Total** | **100%** |

Mike retains near-majority (48.3%) even after four rounds totaling ~$7M raised. With Owen's aligned interests, the founding team controls 64.4% through Series A.

### Comparison: Staged vs. Single Raise

| Approach | Total raised (pre-seed) | Total investor dilution | Mike's ownership after seed |
|----------|------------------------|------------------------|---------------------------|
| **Staged (new)** | $450K ($50K + $400K) | ~7.14% pre-seed + ~9% seed = ~16% | ~56% |
| **Single raise (old)** | $400K at $4.5M | ~8.89% pre-seed + 11.1% seed = ~20% | 56.7% |

The staged approach raises $50K more total capital while preserving comparable or better ownership. The critical difference: Stage 1 ($50K) is closeable in weeks with one check, the founder takes no salary, and the founder already self-funds the entire business (including co-founder compensation) at ~$1,200/month. No survival risk.
